name: aksarallm-7b-dense

estimated_params_breakdown.comment: Computed for tied embeddings: per-layer attn (no bias, GQA 32:8, Q+O = 2*d*n_heads*head_dim = 33,554,432; K+V = 2*d*n_kv*head_dim = 8,388,608) = 41,943,040; per-layer SwiGLU FFN (3 * d * n_inner) = 176,160,768; per-layer norms (2 * d) = 8,192; per-layer total = 218,112,000. 32 layers = 6,979,584,000. Tied embedding (V * d) = 536,870,912. Final RMSNorm = 4,096. Grand total = 7,516,459,008 (~7.52 B). Sized to fit a single v6e-8 (32 GB HBM/chip) with adafactor + NOTHING_SAVEABLE checkpointing + scan_layers, leaving headroom for activations at seq_len=8192.

token_plan.comment: Available corpus is 84.64 B BPE tokens after dedup+tokenize. That is ~11.3x params, comfortably above the 'minimum viable' 2x and slightly below Llama-3-8B's ratio (~12x for the dataset Meta used).

training_plan_v6e8.comment: 1.28M steps * 65,536 tok/step = 84B tokens (~1 epoch over the dedup+tokenized corpus). At an estimated step time of 8-12s on v6e-8 this is roughly 4-6 months wall time. For a faster smoke / first-look run, set max_steps lower (e.g. 30,000 steps = 2B tokens, ~3-4 days).

notes: 7B is the largest dense LLaMA-style model that fits comfortably on a single v6e-8 with adafactor + grad checkpointing.
Use this config when v5p/v6e-32+ TPU is unavailable and you want a complete pretraining run that produces a usable model.
Same vocab and tokenizer as the 20B config (Ezekiel999/aksara-tokenizer-20b) so checkpoints can be evaluated with the same eval harness.
